title: Simple End-of-Bed Storage Bench
summary: A plain low bench sized to sit at the foot of a bed — two 1x10 end panels holding an open bottom shelf between them, trimmed with a 1x2 apron and capped with a 1x12 top.
description:
The two end panels are simple 1x10 rectangles, joined by a pair of 1x10 boards spanning between them near the bottom — this is the bench's storage: an open shelf you can slide shoes, blankets, or a basket onto, not an enclosed bin. Four short 1x2 aprons at each end and four longer 1x2 aprons running the length of the bench tie everything together and keep the frame square.

Keep the end panels square to each other as you attach the shelf and aprons, since a bench this low shows any rack immediately. The 1x12 top caps the whole assembly, sized to slightly overhang the end panels for a finished look.

**This bench is deliberately plain** — open shelf storage rather than bins, drawers, or a lid — which keeps the build to five simple parts and a single afternoon. Size and finish it to match an existing bed frame, since a bench like this reads best as a matched accessory rather than a standalone piece.

materials.name: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

steps.title: Build the panel frame
steps.body: Attach the end and long aprons to the two end panels and the bottom shelf boards, keeping everything square.

steps.title: Attach the top
steps.body: Attach the 1x12 top to the frame, overhanging slightly on all sides.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Sand all surfaces and apply primer and paint, or stain and poly.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

unresolvedImages.alt: Simple bench for the end of a bed
